summary: Full-stack testing of PDF Quote/Devis Signature platform. 7/7 backend pytest cases passed; full Playwright E2E (admin login -> upload -> generate code -> filter -> logout -> signer -> draw signature -> validate -> signed banner) succeeded.
critical_code_review_comments: JWT cookie uses samesite='none' + secure=True which is correct for cross-site preview; ensure production deployment is always HTTPS or cookie will be rejected.
JWT_SECRET falls back to 'dev-secret-change-me' if env var not set — must be set explicitly in production.
ADMIN_PASSWORD env fallback is 'admin123' which is weak; acceptable for MVP but document for prod hardening.
Login endpoint does not implement brute force lockout/rate limiting (playbook recommends 5-fail lockout).
Storing PDFs as base64 in MongoDB is fine for MVP but will hit 16MB BSON limit at ~12MB files; size cap is 20MB which exceeds Mongo doc limit — reduce upload max to ~10MB or migrate to GridFS.

/api/access/file/{code} returns full base64 content publicly with only the code as gate — acceptable per spec but consider adding rate limiting to prevent code bruteforcing (DEV-XXXXX-XX namespace ~2.6B but should still be rate-limited).
_embed_signature_in_pdf reads entire PDF into memory; OK for ≤20MB. No explicit handling if last page has no mediabox quirks (edge case).
seed_data_creation: Backend test creates and deletes its own PDF; Playwright run created 1 file 'playwright_test.pdf' which was signed (status=signed) and left in DB.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Pytest file at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py covers full happy path: auth (success/fail/me/unauth), file upload, list (no _id leak), generate-code (DEV-XXXXX-XX regex), public verify+fetch+sign+double-sign-prevention, status patch, delete. Playwright covered protected route redirect, admin login, PDF upload, code generation, copy code text, filter, search, logout, signer login by code, canvas draw, sign, signed banner.
rca of the issue: No bugs found. All 7 backend tests pass; full Playwright E2E flow signed a document and showed success banner with iframe rendering signed PDF.
